name: Cache Blocking / Tiling
def: Restructuring loops to operate on cache-sized tiles so data is reused before eviction.
problem: Cut memory traffic in algorithms whose working set exceeds cache by maximising reuse.
mechanism: Partition iteration space into blocks that fit L1/L2; process a block fully (reusing loaded lines) before moving on (e.g. tiled matrix multiply).
benefits: Turns DRAM traffic into cache hits
limits: Tile size must be tuned per machine
failures: Wrong tile size (too big spills, too small overhead)
whenNot: Tiny working sets already in cache
myths: "One tile size fits all" — it is hardware-specific
